content_gaps: The concept of a self-purchase is almost entirely absent; the SERPs are dominated by content for gift-givers (coworkers, family). The emotional nuance of buying for oneself to mark this specific transition is a major gap.

The symbolism discussed is generic and outward-facing (compass for 'new journey,' tree of life for 'legacy'). [1] There is no discussion of more private, personal symbols one might choose to mark the internal shift of retirement.

The tone is universally celebratory and congratulatory, using phrases like "celebrate achievements," [7] "well-earned milestone," [5] and "honor years of dedication." [8] This misses the complexity of retirement, which can include feelings of grief, uncertainty, or quiet contemplation alongside celebration. The voice is often sales-y and generic, focused on the "perfect gift." [6]

There is a heavy focus on personalization through engraving names, dates, or stock phrases. [3] The idea of the piece *itself* being inherently meaningful, without needing explicit personalization, is not explored.
